integrations.name: Local Computer
integrations.description: Files, folders, and system access on this Mac

can: Read, write, search, and manage files and folders

integrations.name: Google Workspace

can: Manage email labels and mark as read
Create, read, and update calendar events
limitations: Cannot browse existing Drive files (coming soon)

setupSteps: Click Sign in — your browser opens to Google
Pick your Google account and sign in
You'll see a warning — click Advanced (small text, bottom left), then Go to Delt (unsafe)
Check the boxes for what you want to share, then click Continue
integrations.troubleshooting: The "unsafe" warning is normal — it just means Google hasn't reviewed Delt yet. Your data stays on your computer.

integrations.name: GitHub
integrations.description: Repos, issues, pull requests, code search

can: Create and manage repos, issues, PRs, branches
Read and write files in repos

setupSteps: Click Sign in — your browser opens to GitHub
Log in if you're not already, then click the green Authorize button
integrations.troubleshooting: Pop-up blocked? Allow pop-ups for this page and try again.
tokenSteps: Click here — everything is already filled in
Copy the token (starts with ghp_) and paste it below

integrations.name: Slack

limitations: Cannot create channels or manage workspace settings

setupSteps: Click Sign in — your browser opens to Slack
Pick your workspace, then click Allow
integrations.troubleshooting: Bot not posting? Type /invite @Delt in the channel first.

integrations.name: Notion

limitations: Can only access pages explicitly shared with the integration

setupSteps: Click Sign in — your browser opens to Notion
Pick which pages to share with Delt, then click Allow access
integrations.troubleshooting: Need to share more pages later? Open any page → ··· → Connections → Add Delt.
integrations.tryIt: Search my Notion for meeting notes

integrations.name: Linear
setupSteps: Click here to open Linear settings
Click Create key, name it Delt, and copy the key that appears
Paste it below
integrations.troubleshooting: The key only shows once. If you lost it, delete the old one and create a new one.

integrations.name: Stripe

can: Create and manage customers, invoices, and subscriptions

setupSteps: Click here to open your Stripe dashboard
Click Reveal test key next to "Secret key" and copy it
Paste it below
integrations.troubleshooting: Start with your test key (sk_test_...) to try it safely. Switch to live when you're ready.

integrations.name: Shopify
setupSteps: Click here to open Shopify app settings
Click Create an app, name it Delt, then click Install app
Copy the Admin API access token and paste it below
integrations.troubleshooting: No "Create an app" button? Ask your store owner to enable custom app development first.

integrations.name: HubSpot

can: Create and manage contacts, deals, and tickets

setupSteps: Click Sign in — your browser opens to HubSpot
Log in and click Grant access
integrations.troubleshooting: Pop-up blocked? Allow pop-ups for this page and try again.

integrations.name: Trello

can: Create and manage boards, lists, and cards

setupSteps: Click here to open Trello
Click Allow — a token will appear on screen
Copy the token and paste it below
integrations.troubleshooting: Link not working? Make sure you're logged into Trello first.

integrations.name: Figma

can: Read and inspect design files, frames, and components
Read and post comments on designs
limitations: Cannot create or edit designs on the canvas — Figma has no write API. To create visual designs, build them as HTML/CSS and deploy or screenshot them.

setupSteps: Click Sign in — your browser opens to Figma
Log in and click Allow access
integrations.troubleshooting: Pop-up blocked? Allow pop-ups for this page and try again.
tokenSteps: Click here to open Figma settings
Scroll down to Personal access tokens and click Generate new token
Name it Delt, copy the token, and paste it below

integrations.name: VS Code
integrations.description: Workspace files, diagnostics, terminals, editor control

limitations: Requires VS Code to be running with the MCP extension

setupSteps: Open VS Code, go to Extensions (the puzzle piece icon), search for vscode-mcp, and click Install
Come back here and click Enable
integrations.troubleshooting: Not connecting? Make sure VS Code is open and the extension is installed.
integrations.tryIt: Show errors in my current file

integrations.name: Airtable

can: Create, read, update, and delete records
limitations: Cannot create new bases or tables — only add records to existing ones

setupSteps: Click here to open Airtable token settings
Click Create token, name it Delt, add your bases, and copy the token
Paste it below
integrations.troubleshooting: Token rejected? Make sure you added at least one base when creating the token.
integrations.tryIt: Show records from my first table

integrations.name: Apple Apps

can: Create and manage reminders and notes
limitations: macOS only — requires permission grants on first use

setupSteps: Click Enable below
Your Mac will ask for permission — click OK on each popup
integrations.troubleshooting: Accidentally clicked Don't Allow? Go to System Settings → Privacy & Security and turn on access for Delt.

integrations.name: Microsoft 365

limitations: Cannot create Teams channels or manage Teams settings

setupSteps: Click here to open Azure — sign in with your Microsoft account
Name the app Delt and click Register
Copy the Application (client) ID from the overview page
Paste it below as Client ID — for Tenant ID, type common
integrations.troubleshooting: Not working? Make sure Tenant ID says common (not your email address).

integrations.name: Twilio
setupSteps: Click here to open your Twilio dashboard
Your Account SID, Auth Token, and phone number are all on the main page
Copy each one and paste them into the fields below
integrations.troubleshooting: Texts not sending? Trial accounts can only text verified numbers.
integrations.tryIt: Send a test text to my phone

integrations.name: SendGrid

limitations: Bulk campaigns require verified sender identity

setupSteps: Click here to open SendGrid settings
Click Create API Key, name it Delt, choose Full Access, and click Create & View
Copy the key and paste it below
integrations.troubleshooting: Emails bouncing? Verify your sender address first.
integrations.tryIt: Send a test email to myself

integrations.name: Salesforce

can: Create and manage leads, opportunities, accounts, and contacts

setupSteps: Click Enable — Delt will connect to Salesforce automatically
Your browser will open to Salesforce — sign in and allow access
integrations.troubleshooting: Connection failed? Make sure you have a Salesforce account and try again.

integrations.name: Zoom
setupSteps: Click here to open Zoom's app page
Choose Server-to-Server OAuth, name it Delt
Add meeting:read and meeting:write scopes, then click Activate
Copy the Account ID, Client ID, and Client Secret into the fields below
integrations.troubleshooting: App won't activate? Make sure you added meeting:read and meeting:write scopes.
integrations.tryIt: Schedule a meeting for tomorrow at 2pm

integrations.name: Todoist

can: Add labels, priorities, and due dates

setupSteps: Click here to open Todoist settings
Copy your API token from the page
Paste it below
integrations.troubleshooting: Token not visible? Scroll down on the integrations page — it's under "API token".

integrations.name: Jira
setupSteps: Click here to create an API token
Click Create API token, name it Delt, and copy it
Fill in your email, the token, and your Jira domain below
integrations.troubleshooting: Domain format: yourcompany.atlassian.net (no https://)

integrations.name: Asana

can: Create and manage tasks and subtasks
Set assignees, due dates, and custom fields

setupSteps: Click here to open Asana developer settings
Click Create new token, name it Delt
Copy the token and paste it below
integrations.troubleshooting: Token starts with 1/ followed by numbers. If it doesn't look right, create a new one.

integrations.name: Discord

limitations: Bot must be invited to servers first

setupSteps: Click here to open Discord Developer Portal
Click New Application, name it Delt
Go to Bot tab → Reset Token → copy the token
Paste it below
integrations.troubleshooting: Bot not responding? Make sure you invited it to your server with the right permissions.

integrations.name: Dropbox
setupSteps: Click here to create a Dropbox app
Choose Scoped access, Full Dropbox, name it Delt
Go to Permissions tab and enable file read/write, then generate an access token
Paste the token below
integrations.troubleshooting: Token expired? Dropbox short-lived tokens expire after 4 hours. Generate a new one.

integrations.name: Calendly
setupSteps: Click here to open Calendly API settings
Click Generate new token and copy it
Paste it below
integrations.troubleshooting: No API access? You need a Calendly Professional plan or higher.

integrations.name: Intercom
setupSteps: Click here to open Intercom Developer Hub
Create a new app or select existing, go to Authentication
Copy your Access Token and paste it below
integrations.troubleshooting: Need admin access to your Intercom workspace to create tokens.

integrations.name: Confluence
setupSteps: Click here — same token page as Jira
Create a token if you don't have one, then fill in the fields below
integrations.troubleshooting: Same API token works for both Jira and Confluence if they're on the same Atlassian account.
integrations.tryIt: Search my Confluence for meeting notes

integrations.name: Supabase
setupSteps: Click here to open your Supabase dashboard
Select your project → Settings → API
Copy the Project URL and service_role key (not anon key)
Paste them below
integrations.troubleshooting: Use the service_role key, not the anon key. The anon key has limited access.

integrations.name: Vercel
setupSteps: Click here to open Vercel token settings
Click Create, name it Delt, set scope to your team
Copy the token and paste it below
integrations.troubleshooting: Token only shows once when created. If you lost it, create a new one.

integrations.name: PostgreSQL
integrations.description: Query databases, manage tables, view schemas

can: Run SQL queries (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE)
limitations: Be careful with write queries on production databases

setupSteps: Get your PostgreSQL connection string from your database provider
Format: postgresql://user:password@host:5432/database
Paste it below
integrations.troubleshooting: Connection refused? Make sure your database allows connections from localhost and the port is correct.

integrations.name: Zendesk
setupSteps: Go to your Zendesk admin → Apps and integrations → APIs → Zendesk API
Enable Token Access and click Add API token
Fill in the fields below
integrations.troubleshooting: Subdomain is just the part before .zendesk.com — no dots or full URL.

integrations.name: ClickUp
setupSteps: Click here to open ClickUp app settings
Under API Token, click Generate (or copy existing)
Paste it below
integrations.troubleshooting: Token starts with pk_. If it doesn't, you may be copying the wrong value.

integrations.name: Monday.com

can: Create and update items on boards

setupSteps: Click your avatar in Monday.com → Developers → My access tokens
Copy your API v2 Token
Paste it below
integrations.troubleshooting: Token is a long JWT string starting with eyJ. Make sure you copy the full thing.
